article_meta.title: Christmas travelers left stranded as airports see mass flight cancelations

reasoning: The summary largely avoids the article’s sensational phrasing, replacing it with neutral, data-focused language. It quotes the branded term “Misery Map” but otherwise steers clear of loaded rhetoric. One mild instance of inherited tone appears with the use of “chaos,” but it does not dominate the summary.

custom_reasoning.spin: Nearly all verbs and descriptors are neutral; only a single use of “chaos.”
custom_reasoning.unsubstantiated_claims: All figures and developments are sourced from the article (FlightAware, FAA, Fox Weather).
custom_reasoning.opinion_as_fact: No subjective judgments stated as fact.
custom_reasoning.sensationalism: Avoids the source’s punny/sensational tone; minor carryover with “chaos.”
custom_reasoning.mind_reading: No attributions of intent or feelings.
custom_reasoning.slant: No competing perspectives to favor; coverage is proportional.
custom_reasoning.bias_by_omission: Includes main elements: East Coast impact, specific airport data, prior day’s disruptions, and West Coast context.
custom_reasoning.subjective_adjectives: Limited to neutral descriptors like “widespread,” “less severe.”
custom_reasoning.negativity_bias: Tone mirrors the factual disruption level without amplifying it.
custom_reasoning.elite_populist_bias: Treats official and data sources evenly; no deference or dismissal.
custom_reasoning.framing_neutrality: Noticeably more neutral than source; avoids loaded phrasing.
custom_reasoning.perspective_completeness: Omits the anecdotal traveler quote but captures all major informational angles; minor underrepresentation only.
